title: salix fengiana var. gymnocarpa
intro: Salix fengiana var. gymnocarpa, also known as the 'naked-fruit willow', is a variety of the Salix fengiana plant, a type of willow native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The health benefits of Salix fengiana var. gymnocarpa include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as acting as an antiseptic and antipyretic. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
